faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Find all answers about UAE residence by property investment
faq.contactSupport: Our expert team is here to help you

q1.question: What is the Residence by Property Investment in the UAE?
q1.answer: The Residence by Property Investment (Investor Residence) allows an investor owning real estate in the UAE to obtain a residence permit (e.g. 2 years or 10-year Golden Visa), subject to meeting specific criteria and thresholds.

q2.question: What are the minimum amounts required for 2-year residence and 10-year Golden Visa?
q2.answer:
For 2-year residence: invest in a property worth at least AED 750,000 (or equivalent).
For the 10-year Golden Visa: the minimum investment is approximately AED 2,000,000 (which can be across multiple properties).

q3.question: Can a mortgaged property be accepted for the application?
q3.answer: Yes, a mortgaged property may be accepted, provided you supply a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the bank and proof of the portion of the mortgage you have already paid.

q4.question: Can I combine multiple properties to reach the Golden Visa threshold?
q4.answer: Yes, you may combine the values of several real estate assets to reach the required threshold for the 10-year residence, as long as the total meets the criteria.

q5.question: Is physical presence in the UAE required to apply for residence?
q5.answer: In most cases, yes — you must be physically present in the UAE at the time of application ("in country"), depending on the rules in force at that time.

q6.question: What documents do I need to submit for the application?
q6.answer:
Typical required documents include:
• Valid passport
• Title deed / property certificate
• Compliant ID photo
• Bank NOC (if mortgaged property)
• Police clearance / good conduct certificate
• Health insurance proof
• Proof that title deed is in your name or shared, under legal rules.

q7.question: Can I sponsor my family under this investor residence?
q7.answer: Yes, a valid investor residence (2-year or Golden Visa) often enables you to sponsor your family (spouse, children, sometimes parents) subject to conditions (documentation, eligibility, age, etc.).

q8.question: What happens if the visa application is rejected?
q8.answer: We offer a "satisfied or refunded" guarantee: if your application is rejected, we refund the service fee (provided your dossier was complete and met the announced conditions).

q9.question: Is support / assistance included?
q9.answer: Yes. After eligibility is confirmed, our service includes full support: document verification, submission, and follow-up until the residency permit is issued.

q10.question: How and when do I pay the service fee?
q10.answer: The service fee (AED 3,500) is paid after you confirm eligibility and upload all required documents, via a secure payment module (Stripe / credit card).

q11.question: How long does the application process take?
q11.answer: The processing time varies based on UAE authorities, but typically it ranges from a few weeks to a few months. We provide a status tracker for your application.

q12.question: Can I work or get a business license with this investor residence?
q12.answer: Yes, in many cases you can request a temporary work permit from the UAE Ministry of Human Resources, and obtain a commercial license if you meet the relevant conditions.

q13.question: Can I apply if I'm outside the UAE?
q13.answer: Some regulations require that the application be submitted from within the UAE. If you're abroad, there may be restrictions depending on the local authority rules.

q14.question: Do I have to undergo medical exams / fingerprinting?
q14.answer: Yes, a medical test is often required. Also, for the Emirates ID, biometric procedures like fingerprint scanning may be required, especially on the first application.

q15.question: What if the property is jointly owned with my spouse and not solely in my name?
q15.answer: It's acceptable in many cases: if the property is co-owned with your spouse, the dossier must include a valid marriage certificate recognized by UAE law, in compliance with requirements.
